# AmbiSecure — extended LLM-friendly site index

> Full index of every indexable page on https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/.
> Generated by `tools/build-llms-full.py` from the search index. Companion to [llms.txt](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/llms.txt).

Each entry is `[title](url) — one-line summary`. Summaries come from the page `<meta name="description">`. No PII is exposed; the file is regenerated whenever the site rebuilds.

## Products

- [AmbiSecure BioKey — Biometric USB Security Key | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/biokey/) — Biometric USB security key with on-device fingerprint sensor. FIDO2 + match-on-device. AAL3-eligible. USB-A or USB-C; NFC variant.
- [AmbiSecure Digital Signature Token — Hardware PKI Token | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/digital-signature-token/) — Hardware PKI signature token. On-token RSA/ECC, PKCS#11, X.509. eIDAS-compatible flows. Pilot batches from 50 units.
- [AmbiSecure eSIM Solution — SGP.22 / SGP.32 eUICC + OpenID Connect | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/esim-solution/) — Ambimat eSIM platform: SGP.22 consumer, SGP.32 M2M, automotive eUICC, with optional OpenID Connect applet for end-user identity verification.
- [AmbiSecure FIDO2 Nano-Card Applet — embedded secure-element authenticator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/fido2-nano-sim-applet/) — FIDO2 / CTAP2 applet on a CC EAL5+ secure element, available in a convenient nano-card (4FF) form factor and an MFF2 solderable module for embedded integration. Hardware-backed credentials, ECC P-256, ISO/IEC 7816…
- [AmbiSecure IoT Security Applets — Secure-Element Applets](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-security-applets/) — JavaCard applets for IoT device identity. Provisioning, attestation, signed firmware update, mutual TLS credential storage. CC EAL5+ secure element.
- [AmbiSEC Module — IoT Security Co-Processor | Dual-Domain Hardware-Rooted IoT Security | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-security-coprocessor/) — Dual-domain IoT security co-processor. MCU domain runs the application; an isolated cryptographic security domain holds keys, signs / verifies / encrypts, prevents replay, and authenticates secure OTA. TEC 31318 coverage. Nano-card and MFF2 solderable form factors.
- [AmbiSecure IoT Solution — End-to-End IoT Security Platform | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-solution/) — End-to-end IoT security: secure-element chipset, key manager service, FIDO-enabled enterprise MFA, attestation pipeline. OEM-friendly.
- [AmbiSecure JavaCard Applet Platform — FIDO, PIV, OpenPGP, NDEF | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/javacard-applets/) — JavaCard applet platform: FIDO, PIV, OpenPGP, Door Access, Certificate Storage, NDEF, OpenID Connect, SCP02, biometric variants, IoT Security. Multi-applet on one chip.
- [AmbiSecure OnePass Bio Card — Biometric FIDO2 Smart Card | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-bio-card/) — Biometric FIDO2 smart card with on-card fingerprint match. Template never leaves silicon. No PIN, no reader, no shared secret. Phishing-resistant.
- [AmbiSecure OnePass Card — FIDO2 smart card | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-card/) — The AmbiSecure OnePass Card is a FIDO2 smart card with resident credentials, NFC + USB, brandable form factor — employee ID and authenticator in one card.
- [AmbiSecure OnePass USB Key — FIDO2 hardware security key | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-usb-key/) — The AmbiSecure OnePass USB Key is a FIDO-certified hardware security key — USB-A or USB-C, no battery, optional PIV applet. Phishing-resistant enterprise authentication.
- [AmbiSecure PIV Bio Card — PIV with on-card fingerprint match](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-bio-card/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on a biometric smart card with on-card fingerprint sensor. Three-factor authentication (have / know / are) on a single ID-1 surface. CC EAL5+ secure element.
- [AmbiSecure PIV Nano-Card Applet — PIV applet on a nano-card secure element](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-nano-sim-applet/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on a CC EAL5+ secure element, available in convenient nano-card (4FF) and solderable MFF2 packages for OEM embedded integration. Four certificate slots, RSA + ECC, hardware-backed…
- [AmbiSecure PIV Smart Card — PIV applet on a contact + contactless ID-1 card](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-card/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on an ISO/IEC 7810 ID-1 smart card with contact + contactless interface. Four certificate slots, RSA + ECC, Windows / macOS smart-card logon, PKCS#11 + minidriver. FIPS 201 functional…
- [AmbiSecure PIV USB Key — PIV applet in a USB-A / USB-C key](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-usb-key/) — PIV-compatible JavaCard applet in a USB-A / USB-C key form factor. CCID + WebAuthn dual interface, smart-card logon, PKCS#11, SSH agent forwarding, optional PIN gate. CC EAL5+ secure element.
- [AmbiSecure PKCS Signature Suite — Token + Middleware](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/pkcs-signature-suite/) — Hardware signature token with PKCS#11 / PKCS#15 middleware. Cross-platform integration: Acrobat, Office, Mozilla, OpenSC, Windows BaseCSP.
- [AmbiSecure Secure Mail Suite — Email + Document Signing](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/secure-mail-suite/) — White-label platform for S/MIME email signing, email encryption, PDF/PKCS#7 document signing. Hardware-backed via smart card, PIV, or PKCS#11 token.
- [AmbiSecure Tappable — NFC FIDO2 Authenticator | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/tappable/) — NFC-only FIDO2 authenticator in card, keyfob, and sticker form factors. Mobile-first authentication and contactless physical access.
- [OnePass Platform](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-platform/) — OnePass is a unified hardware-backed identity platform — smart card, USB key, validation server, lifecycle. Built for enterprises that need device-bound rather than syncable identity.
- [Products](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/) — AmbiSecure product catalogue: FIDO2 OnePass smart cards, biometric cards, USB keys, NFC tappable, IoT security chipset, JavaCard applets, digital signature tokens.

## Services

- [AmbiSecure Bio Enrollment App — Match-on-Card Biometric Enrollment | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/bio-enrollment-app/) — Companion app for AmbiSecure biometric authenticators. Match-on-card / match-on-device enrollment. ISO 19794-2 templates, multi-finger, audit-ready.
- [AmbiSecure ePassport Platform Engineering — Backend + PKI](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/epassport-platform/) — End-to-end ePassport platform engineering. Backend, frontend, PKI (CSCA/DSC/PKD), enrolment, personalisation. ICAO 9303-aligned design.
- [AmbiSecure FIDO Validation Server — Multi-tenant FIDO2 SaaS](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/fido-validation-server/) — FIDO-based passwordless authentication platform. Multi-tenant SaaS. JS API + REST. WebAuthn, CTAP2, MDS lookup. Per-tenant policy and attestation verification.
- [AmbiSecure Multi-Card Applet Loading Tool — SCP03 CAP Loader | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/multi-card-applet-loader/) — Factory tool that loads CAP files onto a stack of smart cards over GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 SCP03. Multi-reader, HSM-backed issuer keys, audit log.
- [AmbiSecure NDEF Personalisation Tool — NFC Forum NDEF Programming | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/ndef-personalisation/) — Programs NDEF URL, vCard, or custom records onto AmbiSecure card and token surfaces. NFC Forum Type 4, bulk programming, audit log.
- [AmbiSecure Security Key Manager — Enterprise Key Lifecycle Tool | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/security-key-manager/) — Administrator-facing tool for AmbiSecure key inventory, lifecycle, and audit. Set/Get Master Key, Generate Key Pair, Sign data, audit log, REST API.
- [FIDO Validation Server demo — access posture](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/fido-validation-server/demo/) — FIDO Validation Server demo access is request-based — no public tenant signup, no open SaaS preview. Schedule a guided evaluation with engineering.
- [JavaCard Applet Development](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/javacard-development/) — Full-cycle JavaCard / JCOP applet development. JavaCard 3.0, GlobalPlatform 2.3, JCOP 3.1, PIV, FIDO U2F, FIDO2 CTAP, WebAuthn. CAP files + source delivered.
- [Services](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/) — AmbiSecure engineering services: JavaCard applet development, FIDO Validation Server, tool-chain development (bio enrollment, key manager, applet loader, NDEF personalisation).
- [Tool-Chain Development](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/) — Smart-card and FIDO tool-chain development: bio enrollment, security key manager, multi-card applet loader, NDEF personalisation. SCP03 loading, factory MES integration.

## Solutions

- [Closed-loop ticketing — transit-grade architecture | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/closed-loop-ticketing/) — Reference for closed-loop fare-collection deployments: DESFire credentials, SAM-protected validators, offline transactions, anti-fraud surface, settlement.
- [eSIM Security](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/esim-security/) — eSIM security — eUICC OS / applets, SM-DP+ integration, GSMA SGP.22 (consumer) / SGP.32 (M2M) compliance, security review across the stack.
- [Government Identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/government-identity/) — Government identity programmes with NIST AAL3 / FIPS 140-3 / CC EAL5+ / eIDAS coverage. Sovereign personalisation, sovereign attestation roots, air-gap-tolerant validation.
- [JavaCard Applet Deployment](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/javacard-deployment/) — JavaCard applet design, implementation, verification, personalisation, and field lifecycle. FIDO2, PIV, OpenPGP, EMV, eID, custom applets at scale.
- [Offline authentication | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/offline-authentication/) — Offline-authentication architecture — hardware-rooted credential, per-session crypto, deferred reconciliation. Pattern, principles, and where to apply it.
- [Passwordless & MFA](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/passwordless-mfa/) — Replace passwords + OTP with hardware-bound FIDO2 credentials — phishing-resistant by construction. Reference architecture, migration path, deployment options.
- [Passwordless Enterprise](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/passwordless-enterprise/) — Real passwordless enterprise — what an end-to-end deployment really looks like. Authenticators, provisioning, recovery, federation, attestation policy, lifecycle automation.
- [Phishing-resistant Authentication](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/phishing-resistant-authentication/) — Phishing-resistant MFA has a precise technical meaning — origin-bound, hardware-backed credentials. What WebAuthn buys you, what it doesn't, and how it maps to NIST AAL3 / CISA / OMB M-22-09.
- [Secure Element Integration](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/secure-element-integration/) — Secure-element integration into your product — chip selection, host driver, JavaCard applet, lifecycle. NXP, Infineon, STMicro families. CC EAL5+ secure elements.
- [Secure validator platforms | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/secure-validator-platforms/) — Validator hardware that survives tamper, environmental stress, and unattended deployment. SAM-protected, signed-firmware, field-serviceable.
- [Smart access control | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/smart-access-control/) — Physical access control with FIDO2 + DESFire-class credentials. One card opens doors, signs into laptops, badges into elevators.
- [Smart-Card Personalisation](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/smart-card-personalization/) — Smart-card personalisation as a service or in-country line. SCP03 + HSM-backed issuance for FIDO2 / PIV / EMV / eID applets. CC EAL5+ secure elements.
- [V2X Security Architecture — OBU, RSU, and ITS PKI | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/v2x-security/) — Cryptographic trust architecture for Vehicle-to-Everything infrastructure. EA/AA PKI, pseudonymous certificates, hardware-isolated key storage, HSM-backed manufacturing provisioning. IEEE 1609.2 / ETSI TS 103 097 / ETSI TS 102 941 design alignment.
- [Solutions](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/) — AmbiSecure solutions: passwordless MFA, IoT root of trust, secure provisioning, digital identity, payment security, secure firmware update.
- [Transit ticketing](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/transit-ticketing/) — Closed-loop transit-ticketing deployments — DESFire cards, SAM-protected validators, depot edge, back-office settlement.
- [Workforce Identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/workforce-identity/) — Workforce identity programme — issuance, daily authentication, recovery, off-boarding. Hardware-rooted with attestation-anchored AAGUID policy and IdP-layer federation.

## Technologies

- [V2X PKI: IEEE 1609.2 and ETSI TS 103 097 Certificate Architecture | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/v2x-pki/) — Technical reference for V2X PKI: Enrolment Credentials, Pseudonymous Certificates, certificate lifecycle, Butterfly Key Expansion, hardware binding requirements for connected-vehicle deployments. India TRAI CP 30/04/2026 policy context.
- [Card ↔ Reader ↔ SAM — mutual-authentication architecture | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/card-reader-sam-flow/) — Reference walkthrough of the trust chain that powers closed-loop DESFire deployments — trust zones, key hierarchy, mutual auth flow, offline lifecycle, validator form factors.
- [CTAP2](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/ctap2/) — CTAP2 is the FIDO Alliance Client-To-Authenticator Protocol — the wire format the browser uses to talk to USB-HID, NFC, and BLE roaming authenticators. Commands, transports, PIN/UV protocols.
- [FIDO & WebAuthn](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/fido/) — How FIDO2 / WebAuthn / CTAP2 work — the trust chain, registration, authentication, attestation, AAGUIDs — and the AmbiSecure products and services that ship it.
- [FIDO2](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/fido2/) — FIDO2 is two specs working in concert: WebAuthn (W3C API) and CTAP2 (FIDO Alliance authenticator wire protocol). What's in the family, what each spec does, and how certification levels work.
- [JavaCard](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/javacard/) — What JavaCard actually is — bytecode, lifecycle, CAP files, SCP03 loading, persistent vs transient memory — and where it fits in our products and services.
- [MIFARE DESFire — EV1, EV2, EV3 architecture | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/desfire/) — What changed across DESFire EV1, EV2, EV3 — application/file model, authentication, secure messaging, transaction MAC. Engineering reference, no exploit content.
- [Passkeys](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/passkeys/) — Passkeys are WebAuthn credentials with a specific posture. The BE/BS flags decide whether they're device-bound or syncable. This page is the engineering posture page — what to require, what to accept, what to reject.
- [Secure Access Modules — SAM AV2 / AV3 architecture | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/sam/) — Why closed-loop transit and access systems put issuer keys in a Secure Access Module, not in reader firmware. SAM AV2/AV3 capabilities, trust zones, host channel.
- [Secure elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/secure-elements/) — What a secure element is, how it differs from TPM and HSM, and where AmbiSecure uses them across cards, USB keys, IoT chipsets, and applet platforms.
- [Technologies](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/) — The technologies AmbiSecure builds with: JavaCard, Secure Elements, FIDO, NFC/DESFire, PKI, eSIM, APDU, embedded cryptography.
- [WebAuthn](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/webauthn/) — WebAuthn (W3C) — registration and authentication ceremonies, clientDataJSON, authenticatorData, attestation objects, RP ID rules, BE/BS flags. With diagrams and verification checklists.
- [WebAuthn Attestation](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/attestation/) — WebAuthn attestation — fmt formats, attStmt structure, x5c chain validation, FIDO MDS BLOB integration, AAGUID allow-listing, enterprise attestation. The full deployment guide.

## Standards evolution timelines

- [ePassport evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/epassport/) — A chronological timeline of ePassport standards evolution: ICAO 9303, BAC, PACE, SAC, EAC, multi-application travel documents.
- [FIDO evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/fido/) — A chronological timeline of FIDO standards evolution, from FIDO 1.0 / U2F through CTAP, FIDO2, WebAuthn, and modern multi-device passkeys.
- [OTP and SMS authentication evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/otp-sms/) — A chronological timeline of one-time-password authentication standards: S/Key, RSA SecurID, HOTP, TOTP, SMS OTP, and the FIDO-driven phishing-resistance pivot.
- [PIV evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/piv/) — A chronological timeline of PIV standards evolution — FIPS 201, SP 800-73, derived PIV credentials, and the modern multi-form-factor PIV deployment.
- [Secure-element evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/secure-elements/) — A chronological timeline of secure-element evolution — from SIM cards through embedded SEs, TPMs, eUICCs, and IoT trust chips.
- [Smart-card evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/smart-cards/) — A chronological timeline of smart-card standards and platform evolution: ISO 7816, JavaCard, GlobalPlatform, DESFire, multi-applet smart cards.
- [Standards evolution timelines](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/) — Decade-scale evolution of the standards AmbiSecure builds against — FIDO, PIV, ePassport, OTP, smart cards, WebAuthn, secure elements.
- [WebAuthn and passkey evolution timeline](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/webauthn-passkey/) — A chronological timeline of WebAuthn and passkey evolution — W3C standardisation, browser support, and the platform shift to synced passkeys.

## Engineering references

- [APDU Status Words Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/apdu-status/) — Searchable APDU status word (SW1/SW2) reference — ISO/IEC 7816-4 standard codes plus GlobalPlatform, EMV, and FIDO U2F-specific values.
- [ASN.1 Universal Tags Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/asn1/) — Searchable ASN.1 universal-class tag reference. BOOLEAN, INTEGER, OCTET STRING, OBJECT IDENTIFIER, SEQUENCE, SET, and the string types.
- [DESFire Status Codes Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/desfire/) — Searchable DESFire status code reference — every documented EV1/EV2/EV3 response status with meaning and context.
- [EMV Tags Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/emv-tags/) — Searchable EMV tag reference covering Books 3, 4, and Contactless. Filter by category — card-data, transaction, crypto, capability, DOL, template.
- [FIDO AAGUIDs Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/aaguids/) — Searchable AAGUID directory for FIDO2 / WebAuthn authenticators. Curated subset of well-known consumer and enterprise devices.
- [GlobalPlatform Status Words Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/globalplatform/) — Searchable reference for GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 status words across card-management commands (INSTALL, LOAD, DELETE, GET STATUS).
- [ISO/IEC 7816 CLA Values Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/iso7816/) — Searchable reference for common ISO/IEC 7816-4 CLA byte values — interindustry, proprietary, GlobalPlatform, secure-messaging variants.
- [JavaCard CAP Components Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/javacard-cap/) — Reference for the 12 standard JavaCard CAP file components — Header, Directory, Applet, Import, ConstantPool, Class, Method, StaticField, ReferenceLocation, Export, Descriptor, Debug.
- [NFC NDEF TNF Types Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/nfc/) — Reference for NDEF Type Name Format (TNF) values — well-known, MIME, absolute URI, external types, plus the special and reserved values.
- [References](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/) — Searchable engineering reference databases — APDU status words, EMV tags, ISO 7816 CLA values, COSE algorithms, FIDO AAGUIDs, DESFire status, GlobalPlatform, ASN.1, NDEF, X.509.
- [WebAuthn COSE Algorithms Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/webauthn-cose/) — Searchable reference for COSE algorithm identifiers used in WebAuthn / FIDO2. ES256, EdDSA, RS256, PS256, and the full IANA registry subset.
- [WebAuthn Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/webauthn/) — WebAuthn engineering reference — registration, authentication, attestation, RP ID rules, BE/BS flags, troubleshooting. Developer-portal layout. Static, fast, copy-able code samples.
- [X.509 / Certificate OIDs Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/x509-oids/) — Searchable reference for common X.509 / PKIX OIDs — RDN attribute types, certificate extensions, signature algorithms, FIDO attestation extensions.
- [X.509 Extensions Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/x509-extensions/) — Reference for common X.509 v3 certificate extensions — SKID, AKID, KU, EKU, SAN, BC, CDP, AIA, certificatePolicies, FIDO AAGUID extension.

## Engineering tools

- [AAGUID Lookup](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/aaguid-lookup/) — Look up FIDO2 / WebAuthn AAGUIDs against a curated offline directory of well-known consumer and enterprise authenticators.
- [APDU Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-parser/) — Free, client-side APDU parser. Decode CLA / INS / P1 / P2 / Lc / Le for Case 1-4 (short and extended length), or response APDUs with status word lookup.
- [APDU Script Validator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-script-validator/) — Validate a multi-line APDU script line-by-line for ISO 7816-4 case 1, 2, 3, 4 — short and extended length. Validation only; no execution.
- [APDU Status Dictionary](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/apdu-status-dict/) — Browseable, filterable dictionary of smart-card status words from ISO 7816-4, GlobalPlatform, EMV, and FIDO.
- [ASCII ↔ HEX Converter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ascii-hex/) — Convert ASCII text to / from HEX. Multiple separator styles, copy-friendly. Client-side only.
- [ASN.1 BER/DER Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/asn1-parser/) — Decode any BER/DER-encoded ASN.1 blob into a clickable tree, with X.509 OID lookup. Hex, base64, or PEM input. Client-side.
- [ASN.1 Tree Explorer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/asn1-tree-explorer/) — Walk DER-encoded ASN.1 as a collapsible tree with OID lookup, primitive type recognition, and per-node hex preview. PEM input accepted.
- [ATR Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/atr-parser/) — Free, client-side ATR parser. Decode TS, T0, TA/TB/TC/TD interface bytes, historical bytes, and TCK from any smart-card ATR. Nothing leaves your browser.
- [ATS Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ats-parser/) — Decode ISO/IEC 14443-4 ATS bytes — TL, T0, TA(1)/TB(1)/TC(1), historical bytes. Client-side.
- [authenticatorData Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/authdata-parser/) — Walk WebAuthn authenticatorData — rpIdHash, flags, signCount, AAGUID, credentialId, COSE_Key, extensions.
- [Base64 Certificate Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base64-cert/) — Detect and decode any certificate blob — PEM, base64, or hex — and emit DER bytes plus a clean PEM. Client-side, multi-block.
- [Base64 Encoder / Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base64/) — Encode or decode base64 (standard and URL-safe variants). Client-side, copy-friendly, sample inputs.
- [Base64URL Converter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/base64url/) — Encode and decode base64url (RFC 4648 §5). The format WebAuthn, JOSE, and CTAP2 use everywhere. Bidirectional conversion — base64url, base64, hex, UTF-8.
- [BER Length Visualizer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ber-length/) — Decode and encode BER length octets — short-form, long-form, and indefinite-form. Quick reference for ASN.1 and EMV TLV encoding.
- [CBOR Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cbor-decoder/) — Decode CBOR (RFC 8949) blobs in your browser — integers, byte / text strings, arrays, maps, tags. Friendly for WebAuthn attestation objects, COSE keys, CTAP2 messages.
- [Certificate Chain Viewer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cert-chain/) — Visualise a TLS / PKI certificate chain from a multi-cert PEM bundle. Each cert summarised; chain links validated by issuer ↔ subject. Client-side.
- [Certificate Fingerprint](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cert-fingerprint/) — Compute SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 fingerprints of an X.509 certificate. Uses Web Crypto. Fully client-side.
- [clientDataJSON Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/clientdata-decoder/) — Decode a base64url clientDataJSON. Verifies type, extracts challenge / origin / crossOrigin, and computes SHA-256 — what the authenticator actually signs.
- [CMAC Length Calculator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cmac-length/) — Compute CMAC block count, padded length, and output truncation for AES / TDES. Practical companion for SCP03, DESFire EV2/EV3, and EMV CDA implementers.
- [COSE Key Inspector](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/cose-key/) — Inspect COSE_Key blobs (RFC 8152) — kty, alg, crv, x, y, n, e. Decode the credentialPublicKey from any WebAuthn attestation.
- [Credential ID Inspector](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/credential-id/) — Inspect a WebAuthn credentialId — length, entropy, AAGUID-shaped prefix, encoding cross-checks. Diagnostic only; credentialIds are opaque to the RP.
- [CSR Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/csr-decoder/) — Decode a PKCS#10 Certificate Signing Request — subject, public key, requested extensions, signature algorithm. Client-side only, no upload.
- [DESFire Access-Rights Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-access-rights/) — Decode the 16-bit DESFire access-rights word into Read / Write / Read+Write / ChangeAccessRights key references.
- [DESFire File Settings Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-file-settings/) — Decode the DESFire GetFileSettings response — file type, communication settings, access rights word, per-type fields (Std / Backup / Value / Linear Record / Cyclic Record / TMAC).
- [DESFire Key Settings Interpreter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-key-settings/) — Decode the 2-byte DESFire GetKeySettings response — keySettings byte (ChangeKey / config / master-key flags), numKeys, and crypto type (DES / 3DES / AES).
- [DESFire Status Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/desfire-status/) — Look up DESFire response status bytes — OPERATION_OK, AUTHENTICATION_ERROR, ADDITIONAL_FRAME, etc. Client-side reference.
- [EMV Tag Dictionary](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/emv-tag-dict/) — Filterable directory of EMV (Books 3, 4, and CL) tags — SELECT, GET PROCESSING OPTIONS, READ RECORD, GENERATE AC, terminal capabilities.
- [Endian Converter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/endian/) — Convert between big-endian (BE) and little-endian (LE) for u16 / u32 / u64 hex blocks. Shows unsigned numeric value too.
- [FIDO Metadata Explorer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/fido-mds-explorer/) — Browse the AAGUID directory in metadata-style cards — vendor, model, transports, BE=0 posture. Searchable. Production should fetch the FIDO MDS BLOB; this is an offline stand-in.
- [GlobalPlatform Status Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/gp-status/) — Reference for GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 — card lifecycle states, application lifecycle states, ISD permissions.
- [HEX ↔ Byte-Array](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/hex-bytes/) — Convert hex strings into C / Java / Python / JS byte arrays — and back. Multiple programmer formats. Client-side only.
- [ISO 7816-4 CLA Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso7816-cla/) — Decode the ISO/IEC 7816-4 CLA byte — interindustry vs proprietary, secure messaging bits, command chaining, logical channel.
- [ISO/IEC 14443 Frame Explorer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso14443-frame/) — Decode ISO/IEC 14443-3 (REQA / WUPA / ATQA / SAK) and 14443-4 (RATS / I-block / R-block / S-block) frame structures.
- [ISO/IEC 14443 Quick Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso14443-ref/) — ISO/IEC 14443 contactless smart cards: parts, Type A vs B, anti-collision, framing, I/R/S blocks. One-page reference.
- [ISO/IEC 7816 Quick Reference](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/iso7816-ref/) — A bookmark-friendly quick reference card for ISO/IEC 7816 — APDU structure, common INS bytes, file types, security messaging.
- [JavaCard CAP Structure Explorer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/javacard-cap/) — Explore JavaCard CAP file components (Header, Directory, Applet, Import, ConstantPool, Class, Method, StaticField, RefLocation, Export, Descriptor, Debug). Decode Header.cap magic / version / package AID.
- [Key Diversification Visualizer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/key-diversification/) — Educational visualizer for AES-CMAC key diversification (NXP AN10922 / AV2). Shows the SHAPE of the derivation, not a production-grade computation.
- [Length-Field Encoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/length-field/) — Encode an integer length in BER long-form, DGI long-form, 1-byte, and 2-byte BE/LE. Useful for APDU and TLV building.
- [NDEF Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/ndef-decoder/) — Decode NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) messages — URI, Text, Smart Poster, MIME, External records.
- [Passkey Flow Visualizer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/passkey-flow-viz/) — Step-by-step walkthrough of the WebAuthn registration and authentication ceremonies, with annotation linking to the relevant decoder tools.
- [PEM ↔ DER Converter](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/pem-der/) — Free, client-side PEM ↔ DER converter for certificates, CSRs, public/private keys. PEM block detection, multi-block support, downloadable output.
- [PFX / PKCS#12 Inspector](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/pfx-inspector/) — Inspect the structure of a .pfx / .p12 PKCS#12 archive — bag types, encryption schemes, embedded certificates. Client-side; password-decryption flagged as a current limitation.
- [RP ID / Origin Validator](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/rp-id-validator/) — Validate WebAuthn rpId / origin combinations. Confirms registrable-suffix rule, HTTPS posture, public-suffix-list constraints.
- [SCP03 Session Walkthrough](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/scp03-walkthrough/) — Educational walkthrough of GlobalPlatform SCP03 (AES-128 mutual authentication and secure messaging). No production key compute — illustration only.
- [Secure Messaging Field Visualizer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/secure-messaging-viz/) — Walk SM-wrapped APDU body data — DOs 87 (cryptogram), 97 (Le), 99 (status), 8E (MAC), 84 (legacy cryptogram). Reference for ISO 7816-4 secure-messaging implementers.
- [SW1/SW2 Lookup](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/sw-lookup/) — Look up smart-card status words (SW1 SW2) by hex or by phrase. ISO 7816-4, GlobalPlatform, EMV, FIDO common returns.
- [TLV Parser](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/tlv-parser/) — Free, client-side BER-TLV parser with an EMV tag dictionary. Walks tag/length/value trees of any depth. Nothing leaves your browser.
- [TLV Tree Visualizer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/tlv-tree-viz/) — Visualise BER-TLV / EMV TLV trees as collapsible nested nodes with EMV tag-name lookup. The visual companion to the TLV parser.
- [Transaction MAC Field Visualizer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/tmac-visualizer/) — Visualise the DESFire EV2/EV3 Transaction MAC field — 4-byte counter (TMC) + 8-byte MAC value (TMV). Educational reference for transit / closed-loop ticketing implementers.
- [UID Analyzer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/uid-analyzer/) — Inspect ISO/IEC 14443 Type A UID — cascade level, manufacturer, fixed vs random.
- [UTF-8 Inspector](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/utf8/) — See exactly how UTF-8 encodes a string — codepoints, byte counts, BOM detection, per-character breakdown.
- [WebAuthn Attestation Decoder](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/attestation-decoder/) — Decode WebAuthn attestationObject (CBOR with fmt, attStmt, authData). Walks attStmt and links into the authenticatorData parser.
- [WebAuthn Challenge Viewer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/challenge-viewer/) — Inspect a WebAuthn challenge — length, Shannon entropy, common pitfalls. Generate fresh 32-byte challenges with the platform CSPRNG.
- [X.509 Certificate Viewer](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/tools/x509-viewer/) — Decode an X.509 certificate (or chain) into subject, issuer, validity, public key, signature algorithm, and all extensions — fully client-side, no upload.

## Engineering blog (modern)

- [APDU from First Principles: CLA, INS, P1/P2, Le, Lc, and SW1/SW2 | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/apdu-from-first-principles/) — A practical, deployment-focused walkthrough of ISO/IEC 7816-4 APDUs — Case 1-4, short and extended length, CLA bits, status words, and the gotchas. With code that compiles.
- [Building Secure IoT Identity with Security Applets](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/secure-iot-identity-with-applets/) — Five JavaCard applets for IoT device identity: provisioning, attestation, mTLS, signed firmware, key rotation. Architecture on a CC EAL5+ secure element.
- [Cyber Security Threats — What Actually Matters in 2026 | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/cyber-security-threats-overview/) — A modern, clear-eyed overview of the cyber-security threats that drive identity and hardware-credential decisions in 2026 — phishing, MFA bypass, SIM swap, credential stuffing, device-cloning.
- [DESFire EV1 vs EV2 vs EV3 — Architectural Evolution | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/desfire-ev1-vs-ev2-vs-ev3/) — What actually changed across DESFire EV1, EV2, and EV3 — secure messaging, transaction MAC, key diversification, side-channel hardening. Architecture-level pillar post.
- [Designing Enterprise Passwordless Systems — the deployment runbook](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/designing-enterprise-passwordless-systems/) — What a passwordless rollout actually looks like at workforce scale. Nine load-bearing components, the recovery flow, attestation policy, lifecycle automation, and the migration plan.
- [Designing Low-Latency Secure Transit Validators | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/designing-low-latency-secure-transit-validators/) — How to keep tap-to-decision under 300 ms while running EV2 mutual auth, transaction MAC, and audit writes. Engineering disciplines that decide whether the deployment ships.
- [Designing Secure Credential Lifecycle Management | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/credential-lifecycle-management/) — Credentials don't just get issued and used — they get re-issued, rotated, recovered, and revoked. A practical guide to the lifecycle every hardware-credential programme has to design.
- [Designing Secure Email and Document Signing Platforms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/designing-email-document-signing-platforms/) — Architecture for S/MIME email signing, PDF/PKCS#7 document signing, hardware-backed credentials, Long-Term Validation, and the trust-list problem.
- [Embedded Secure-Element FIDO2 Authenticators for Enterprise Identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/sim-based-fido2-authenticators/) — How embedded secure-element FIDO2 applets work as roaming or embedded authenticators. How nano-card and MFF2 secure elements act as roaming or embedded FIDO2 authenticators, the threat-model gains, and the enterprise…
- [Engineering Blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/) — Practical, code-first writing on FIDO, WebAuthn, JavaCard, DESFire, SAM-backed transit, passwordless rollouts, and smart-card engineering.
- [Engineering blog — page 2](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/page/2/) — Older posts from the AmbiSecure engineering blog. Pagination scaffold; modern blog currently fits on page 1.
- [Engineering ePassport Issuance and Identity Platforms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/engineering-epassport-issuance-platforms/) — Architecture of an ICAO 9303 ePassport platform: CSCA/DSC PKI, LDS generation, SOd signing, enrolment frontend, personalisation backbone, inspection-system reference.
- [How FIDO Authentication Works | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/how-fido-authentication-works/) — A clear, non-academic explanation of how FIDO2 / WebAuthn actually works — registration, authentication, origin binding, what the device does, what the server does. Written for people who want to understand the system…
- [How to Choose Between Smart Cards, FIDO Tokens and Passkeys | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/smart-cards-vs-fido-tokens-vs-passkeys/) — A decision-grade comparison of smart-card authenticators, dedicated FIDO security keys, and passkeys. Threat model, lifecycle, recovery, and the choice that fits each deployment.
- [Implementing FIDO2 Authentication: A Complete Developer Guide | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/implementing-fido2-developer-guide/) — A practical, deployment-focused walkthrough of FIDO2 — CTAP2, WebAuthn, attestation, registration, authentication. Code that compiles, choices that scale.
- [JavaCard Applet Development for Enterprise Identity | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/javacard-applet-development-enterprise-identity/) — JavaCard applets are how custom enterprise-identity behaviour lives on a smart card. A practical guide to building applets that ship: AID design, lifecycle, secure messaging, personalisation, and the mistakes to avoid.
- [Passkeys vs Traditional MFA — what really changed](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/passkeys-vs-traditional-mfa/) — Passkeys are not MFA-just-stronger. They are a different cryptographic primitive that retires phishing, replay, credential stuffing, and push fatigue as attack classes.
- [PIV Smart Cards vs USB Tokens vs Embedded Secure Elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/piv-vs-usb-tokens-vs-embedded/) — Workforce identity credential matrix: PIV smart card vs USB token vs embedded secure element. Lifecycle, physical-logical convergence, certificate workflows, deployment realities.
- [PKI Credential Issuance for Workforce and Government Identity | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/pki-credential-issuance-workforce-government/) — Issuing X.509 credentials at workforce or government scale is more architecture than cryptography. RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit. What gets built and what gets bought.
- [Platform vs Roaming Authenticators — picking the right form factor](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/platform-vs-roaming-authenticators/) — Platform and roaming FIDO2 authenticators look interchangeable on a slide and behave differently in production. The decision matrix, the form-factor tradeoffs, and the right hybrid mix.
- [Secure Element vs TPM vs HSM — Where Each Fits | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/secure-element-vs-tpm-vs-hsm/) — Three classes of hardware key-storage that everyone confuses. What each is actually for, what each refuses to do, and how to choose for device, workstation, or backend.
- [Top 3 Benefits of Multi-factor Authentication | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/top-3-benefits-of-mfa/) — The three reasons MFA isn't optional any more — and what changes when the second factor is rooted in hardware rather than an OTP seed in someone's phone.
- [Understanding WebAuthn Attestation Objects — the engineering walkthrough](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/understanding-webauthn-attestation-objects/) — WebAuthn attestation explained as engineering, not spec text. The seven formats, the verification pipeline, the MDS BLOB, AAGUID allow-lists, and the pitfalls that bite in production.
- [Where Your AmbiSecure FIDO Key Works | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/fido-supported-services/) — A current, vendor-neutral overview of the services and platforms that accept FIDO2 / WebAuthn authentication — cloud SaaS, OS-level sign-in, IdPs, and developer platforms.
- [Why Hardware-Backed Identity Matters — the cost-per-extraction frame](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-hardware-backed-identity-matters/) — Hardware moves the cost-per-extraction of an authentication credential up by orders of magnitude. The threat surfaces software cannot defend, and what hardware actually changes.
- [Why SAMs Matter in Closed-Loop Transit Systems | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-sams-matter-in-closed-loop-transit/) — Why every closed-loop transit deployment over a few thousand cards needs a Secure Access Module. Containment, diversification, auditable issuance, host channel.
- [Why Transit Validators Need Offline Trust Architecture | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/transit-validators-offline-trust-architecture/) — Closed-loop transit ticketing has to keep collecting fares when the backend is unreachable. This is the architecture that lets validators do the right thing without phoning home.
- [Why use Multi-factor Authentication? | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/why-use-multi-factor-authentication/) — Cybercriminals have billions of leaked credentials. A second factor — especially a hardware-bound one — cuts off the long tail of credential-stuffing and phishing.

## Engineering archive (2017–2025)

- [An Introduction to Java Card Technology (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/introduction-to-java-card/) — An introduction to JavaCard — how Java-based applets run securely on smart cards and other small-memory secure elements, why the JavaCard runtime model exists, and what it enables for FIDO, PIV, and ePassport applets.
- [Challenges to IoT Security (Part 1) (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/iot-security-challenges-part-1/) — First of a two-part look at IoT security — how billions of devices communicate with enterprise systems, where the resulting attack surface sits, and the trust anchors that contain it.
- [Challenges to IoT Security (Part 2) (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/iot-security-challenges-part-2/) — Second of a two-part look at IoT security — practical mitigations: hardware root of trust, signed firmware update, attestation, and device identity carried in a secure element.
- [Common Misconceptions about Two-Factor Authentication (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/common-misconceptions-about-2fa/) — Unpacks the most common misconceptions about two-factor authentication — what 2FA actually defends against, where SMS-based codes fall short, and why hardware-rooted second factors hold up under credential-theft attacks.
- [Consumer Biometrics in the Data Privacy Age (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/consumer-biometrics-and-privacy/) — How organisations can deploy biometric authentication while still meeting modern privacy expectations — match-on-device versus match-on-server, template storage, and the regulatory shape of consumer biometric data.
- [Cyber Attacks in India (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/cyber-attacks-in-india-part-1/) — Part one of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India and what they reveal about systemic gaps in online identity, authentication, and incident response.
- [Cyber Attacks in India – Part 2 (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/cyber-attacks-in-india-part-2/) — Part two of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — the attack patterns, the identity weaknesses they exploited, and what enterprise authentication posture would have stopped them.
- [Cyber Attacks in India – Part 3 (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/cyber-attacks-in-india-part-3/) — Part three of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — closing the series with systemic gaps in incident response, identity hygiene, and hardware-rooted authentication adoption.
- [Engineering Archive](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/) — AmbiSecure engineering archive: FIDO, MFA, JavaCard, smart cards, transit, IoT security pieces from 2017 onwards, with links forward to current coverage.
- [Enterprise Security Threats (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/enterprise-security-threats/) — A field overview of enterprise security threats in the cloud era — how the digital attack surface expands as organisations adopt new platforms, and where hardware-rooted identity narrows it.
- [ePassport and How will chip-based e-Passports work (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/how-chip-based-epassports-work/) — How chip-based ePassports work in practice — the BAC / PACE access protocols, the CSCA / DSC / PKD trust chain, and the role of the secure element inside the document.
- [ePassport and Its Application (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/epassport-applications/) — How biometric passports work in practice — chip-based identity, border-control architecture, and the standards stack that keeps the issuer chain trustworthy.
- [Fast Identity Online (FIDO) (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/fast-identity-online/) — A primer on FIDO (Fast Identity Online) and the Universal Second Factor model — how FIDO replaces shared secrets with hardware-bound credentials and why it changes the phishing-resistance baseline.
- [Is Passwordless the future? (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/is-passwordless-the-future/) — Examines whether passwordless authentication can scale beyond pilots — the standards, attack-surface trade-offs, and operational realities that decide its trajectory.
- [Multi-factor Authentication in Government Sector (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/mfa-in-government/) — Why government identity programmes are adopting multi-factor authentication, how MFA holds up against phishing and credential theft, and where hardware-rooted authenticators sit in the stack.
- [Public Transport Ticketing System (Part-1) (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/public-transport-ticketing-part-1/) — First of three articles on automated fare collection — a comparative look at ticketing architectures across advanced economies and large transit networks in Asia.
- [Public Transport Ticketing System (Part-2) (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/public-transport-ticketing-part-2/) — Part two of the AFC series — how Ambimat Electronics has approached automated fare collection: validators, SAM-backed offline trust, and revenue assurance.
- [Public Transport Ticketing System (Part-3) (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/public-transport-ticketing-part-3/) — Part three of the AFC series — comparative notes across the analysed countries, what was common in mature ticketing architectures, and where hardware-rooted secure access modules sit in the stack.
- [Securing your IIoT infrastructure (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/securing-iiot-infrastructure/) — Why industrial IoT infrastructure remains a high-value target — the markets driving adoption, the trust gaps in legacy OT, and how hardware-rooted device identity tightens them.
- [Single Sign-On Vs. Multi-Factor Authentication (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/single-sign-on-vs-mfa/) — Compares single sign-on with multi-factor authentication — when each model fits, where they overlap, and how SSO + MFA combine for enterprise identity.
- [SMS-based OTP Authentication and Its Disadvantages (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/sms-otp-disadvantages/) — Why SMS-based one-time passwords are no longer a defensible second factor — SIM-swap attacks, SS7 weaknesses, phishing-prone OTP relay, and the hardware-rooted alternatives.
- [Taking security future for Government (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/security-future-for-government/) — How governments are moving from password-only access to multi-factor authentication for citizen and employee identity — and where hardware-rooted credentials reshape the threat model.
- [Understanding EMV certification In Public Transportation (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/emv-certification-in-public-transport/) — A practical look at EMV certification in public transport — which certifications apply, the roles of acquirer, scheme, terminal vendor, and transit authority, and the typical timeline from prototype to revenue service.
- [What is Passwordless Authentication? (Archive 2021)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/what-is-passwordless-authentication/) — A primer on passwordless authentication — what it actually means, how FIDO and biometrics replace shared secrets, and why hardware-bound credentials sit at the centre of the model.
- [Workplace Security — How Biometrics Is the Key to the New Normal (Archive 2020)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/workplace-biometrics/) — How workplace biometrics layer additional security onto existing authentication methods — face / fingerprint / palm, the privacy controls that keep them defensible, and where they sit alongside FIDO and MFA.

- [Passwordless](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/categories/passwordless/) — All AmbiSecure engineering blog content under Passwordless, modern and archive.
- [Privacy](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/categories/privacy/) — All AmbiSecure engineering blog content under Privacy, modern and archive.
- [Smart Cards](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/categories/smart-cards/) — All AmbiSecure engineering blog content under Smart Cards, modern and archive.
- [Transit](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/categories/transit/) — All AmbiSecure engineering blog content under Transit, modern and archive.
- [WebAuthn](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/categories/webauthn/) — All AmbiSecure engineering blog content under WebAuthn, modern and archive.

## Blog tags

- [Browse the blog by tag](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/tags/) — Every tag across the AmbiSecure engineering blog — 23 topic clusters after the Phase 13 taxonomy cleanup.
- [Posts tagged "AAGUID"](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/tags/aaguid/) — 2 AmbiSecure engineering blog posts tagged AAGUID. Latest: How to Choose Between Smart Cards, FIDO Tokens and Passkeys (2026-05-11).
- [Posts tagged "Attestation"](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/tags/attestation/) — 2 AmbiSecure engineering blog posts tagged Attestation. Latest: How to Choose Between Smart Cards, FIDO Tokens and Passkeys (2026-05-11).
- [Posts tagged "Biometrics"](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/tags/biometrics/) — 2 AmbiSecure engineering blog posts tagged Biometrics. Latest: Workplace Security — How Biometrics Is the Key to the New Normal (2020-07-30).
- [Posts tagged "Cyber Security"](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/tags/cyber-security/) — 8 AmbiSecure engineering blog posts tagged Cyber Security. Latest: Cyber Security Threats — What Actually Matters in 2026 (2026-05-11).

## Case studies

- [Case studies](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/) — Anonymised architecture write-ups from real AmbiSecure deployments — passwordless workforce, transit ticketing, secure device identity.
- [Closed-loop transit ticketing](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/transit-ticketing/) — DESFire EV2 + SAM-backed validator architecture for a metro operator's 700+ gate closed-loop ticketing fleet.
- [Passwordless workforce authentication](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/passwordless-workforce/) — How a 9,000-seat workforce migrated from SMS and TOTP to FIDO2 across Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, and a legacy CAS portal.
- [Secure device identity for IoT](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/secure-device-identity/) — Adding hardware-rooted device identity to a connected-device line: secure element on the BOM, SMT-line personalisation, and PKI lifecycle.

## Industries

- [Connected Mobility & V2X Security | AmbiSecure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/connected-mobility/) — Hardware-backed identity for V2X infrastructure. Secure-element integration for OBUs and RSUs, V2X PKI architecture, and ITS certificate lifecycle management for India's connected road ecosystem. IEEE 1609.2 / ETSI TS 103 097 / ETSI TS 102 941 / ISO 21177 / TEC 31318:2021 design alignment.
- [Enterprise access](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/enterprise-access/) — Combined physical access + passwordless authentication using a single FIDO2 + DESFire-class card. Pilot to deployment in months.
- [Government & Defence](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/government-and-defence/) — PIV-style smart cards, eID, digital signature tokens, custom JavaCard applets for government and defence deployments. Audit-grade issuance.
- [Industries](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/) — Where AmbiSecure ships: banking and payments, government and defence, telecom, healthcare, IoT and industrial, retail and hospitality.
- [Smart cities](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/smart-cities/) — Multi-application DESFire credentials for transit + city services. SAM-based key custody that survives an inter-departmental procurement model.
- [Transportation](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/industries/transportation/) — Closed-loop fare collection, secure validator hardware, SAM-protected key custody for metros, BRT, light rail, ferries, and bus fleets.

## Videos

- [AmbiSecure BioKey — product hero loop](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/biokey-product-loop/) — A short product hero loop showing the AmbiSecure BioKey biometric USB security key.
- [AmbiSecure card — all use cases](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/ambisecure-card-use-cases/) — A 1m 27s overview of the AmbiSecure smart card across FIDO, PIV, door access, NDEF, and OpenPGP roles — what the multi-application card can carry.
- [AmbiSecure Videos — FIDO setup walkthroughs and product demos](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/) — AmbiSecure tutorial videos: FIDO U2F setup on Gmail, Facebook, mobile; AmbiSecure card use-case overview; BioKey product loop.
- [How to set up the AmbiSecure card on desktop (U2F / FIDO)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-desktop/) — A 1m 15s walkthrough of registering the AmbiSecure smart card as a FIDO U2F authenticator on a desktop browser.
- [How to set up the AmbiSecure card on Facebook (U2F / FIDO)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-facebook/) — Pair a hardware FIDO key with a Facebook account in under 90 seconds. Covers the security key tab under settings, registration, and verification.
- [How to set up the AmbiSecure card on Gmail (U2F / FIDO)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-gmail/) — Pair the AmbiSecure card with a Google account. Walkthrough of 2-step verification → security key registration on a desktop browser.
- [Set up the AmbiSecure card on your phone — Facebook](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-mobile-facebook/) — Quick 24-second clip showing the NFC tap-and-register flow for the AmbiSecure card with the Facebook mobile app.
- [Set up the AmbiSecure card on your phone — Gmail](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/videos/setup-ambisecure-card-mobile-gmail/) — A 1m 15s walkthrough of NFC-tap registration of the AmbiSecure card with a Google account from a mobile device.

## Brochures

- [Brochures](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/) — Downloadable / printable overviews of each AmbiSecure platform — OnePass, FIDO, transit security, JavaCard, PKI, device identity.
- [Device identity / IoT brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/device-identity/) — Hardware-rooted device identity for fleet-class IoT — secure element on the BOM, SMT-line personalisation, EST-based rotation.
- [FIDO deployment brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/fido/) — What an enterprise FIDO2 rollout actually looks like — AAGUID policy, attestation, recovery, common deployment mistakes.
- [JavaCard platform brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/javacard/) — Multi-applet JavaCard platform — FIDO2, PIV, OpenPGP, NDEF, biometric variants, IoT applets, custom enterprise applets, AID-selectable.
- [OnePass platform brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/onepass/) — OnePass platform overview — smart card, USB key, bio card, validation server, lifecycle. Engineered for hardware-backed identity.
- [PKI & credential lifecycle brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/pki/) — Workforce and government-scale PKI — RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit. Designed to be defensible.
- [Transit security brochure](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/brochures/transit-security/) — Closed-loop transit ticketing with DESFire EV2/EV3, SAM-backed validators, offline trust, and sub-300 ms tap-and-go.

## Engagement models

- [Engagement models](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/engagement-models/) — How AmbiSecure engagements are scoped — from architecture reviews to multi-quarter rollouts. No published price list; engagements are scoped to the problem.

## About

- [About](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/about/) — AmbiSecure is the security business unit of Ambimat Electronics. Forty years of embedded systems experience, focused on FIDO, JavaCard, Secure Element, and IoT trust.
- [Certifications & Standards](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/about/certifications/) — AmbiSecure certifications, standards conformance, and trust posture. FIDO Alliance, JavaCard 3.x, GlobalPlatform 2.3.1, ISO 7816, ISO 14443, NIST SP 800-73, certification targets and disclaimers.

## Resources

- [Resources](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/) — A toolbox of free, client-side utilities for smart-card, secure-element, and telecom engineers. ATR, APDU, TLV, ASN.1, CBOR, ICCID, NDEF, SCP03 — all in your browser.

## Contact

- [Contact AmbiSecure — Talk to engineers, not BDRs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/contact/) — Get in touch with AmbiSecure. India and US sales lines, engineering inquiries, partnership requests. We respond from Ahmedabad, Gujarat.

## Support

- [Support](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/support/) — Support hub for AmbiSecure customers — FAQs, integration guides, datasheets, and direct lines into engineering.

## Partners

- [Partners](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/partners/) — AmbiSecure partner programme: resellers, OEMs, system integrators, MSSPs, and FIDO validation server white-label partners. Hardware-rooted security with co-branded options.

## Trust

- [Trust Center](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/trust/) — AmbiSecure Trust Center: certification posture, standards conformance, security model, disclosure practice, and trust documents on request.

## Privacy

- [Privacy & analytics preferences](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/privacy/) — Opt-out of analytics, review what AmbiSecure collects, and adjust your in-browser preference. DNT respected by default.

## Other

- [AmbiSecure — Secure Element, FIDO & JavaCard engineering for connected systems](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/) — AmbiSecure builds hardware-rooted identity, FIDO authenticators, JavaCard applets, and IoT trust systems. Engineering-led, deployment-focused, ecosystem-aware.
- [FAQs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/faqs/) — Answers to the questions engineering teams ask about AmbiSecure hardware-rooted identity — FIDO2 security keys, PIV smart cards, JavaCard applets, secure elements, eSIM authentication, PKI infrastructure, and…
- [Search AmbiSecure — products, services, technologies, blog](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/search/) — Sitewide client-side search across products, services, technologies, solutions, industries, case studies, references, the engineering blog, and the engineering archive. No backend, no third-party tracking.
